title: In the field of marketing research, analytics exclusively uses qualitative techniques to determine performance efficienc…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
In the field of marketing research, analytics exclusively uses qualitative techniques to determine performance efficiency.

True or False

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: False. Analytics uses both qualitative and quantitative techniques to determine performance efficiency in marketing research.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Creating customer profiles and understanding behavioral characteristics are major focuses of any marketing research project.

True or False

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: True. Creating customer profiles and understanding behavioral characteristics are important aspects of marketing research projects as they help in identifying the target audience and developing effective marketing strategies.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
________ is information collected specifically for a current research problem or opportunity.
Multiple Choice
Primary data
Alternate data
Secondary data
Warehouse data
Backup data

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following statements is true about the information research process?
Multiple Choice
It narrows the applicability of the research process in solving organizational problems and creating opportunities.
A more appropriate name for the information research process is now the traditional marketing research process.
It is a systematic approach to collecting, analyzing, interpreting, and transforming data into decision-making information.
It is especially useful when costs outweigh the value of the research.
It serves as a written contract between the decision maker and the researcher.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: It is a systematic approach to collecting, analyzing, interpreting, and transforming data into decision-making information.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following is a characteristic of management decision makers?
Multiple Choice
Their dominant concern is market performance.
They prefer abstractions over information that allows for certainty.
They tend to readily accept information when they are surprised.
Their dominant concern is to conduct continuous inquiries into market component changes.
Their dominant concern is to ensure completeness by conducting prolonged investigations.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Their dominant concern is market performance.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
The iceberg principle in marketing research holds that ________.
Multiple Choice
marketing decision makers are aware of only 10 percent of the true problem in research
marketing decision makers usually have a general idea of why research is needed
marketing decision makers are unaware of the symptoms of a research problem
invisible research problems should be omitted from the problem definition
decisions based only on the symptoms of a research problem are usually correct

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Marketing decision makers usually have a general idea of why research is needed.
